title: Support figure and implementation document types in Web UI document viewer
description:
Extend the Web UI document viewer to render image files and Python/script files, which appear as linked documents on issues from other JIT projects (e.g. `figure` and `implementation` doc types).

## Background

The document viewer renderer registry currently recognises `.csv`, `.txt`, `.rs`, and `.cpp` by extension, with a markdown catch-all. Two document types found on issues in associated projects are not handled:

- **Figure documents** (`.png`, `.svg`, `.jpg`) -- rendered as raw text or broken markdown because no image renderer exists.
- **Implementation scripts** (`.py`, and other common script extensions) -- fall through to the markdown catch-all, losing syntax highlighting.

The `DocumentReference` type already carries a `doc_type` field (`string | undefined`), which `pickRenderer` can use as a secondary matching signal alongside file extension.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `.png`, `.svg`, and `.jpg` files render as inline images in the document viewer (not as raw text or broken markdown).
- [ ] `.py` files render with syntax highlighting via `TextCodeRenderer` or an equivalent renderer.
- [ ] `pickRenderer` matches image extensions (and optionally `doc_type === "figure"`) before falling through to the catch-all.
- [ ] `pickRenderer` matches `.py` (and optionally other common script extensions: `.sh`, `.js`, `.ts`) before falling through to the catch-all.
- [ ] All new renderer paths have unit test coverage in `renderers.test.tsx`.
- [ ] `npm-ci` gate passes (vitest, ESLint, build).
